report_name: FBI Links "First VPN Service" to Ransomware Gangs and Dark Web Activity

attack_patterns.name: Multi-hop Proxy
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors use the "First VPN Service" to route their malicious traffic through multiple hops, obscuring their true location and making attribution more difficult.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Cybercriminals use compromised credentials to access remote services such as corporate VPNs, SSH, and RDP, while using the "First VPN Service" to provide anonymity for the access attempts.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Attackers target legitimate external remote services like corporate VPNs, SSH, and RDP for initial access, using the "First VPN Service" to hide their origin.

tools.name: First VPN Service
tools.description: A virtual private network provider identified by the FBI as being actively used by cybercriminals, including ransomware gangs and botnet operators, to anonymize their malicious activities and bypass security controls.

identities.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation
identities.description: Issued a public security advisory warning about the "First VPN Service" and its use by cybercriminals.

identities.name: Internet Crime Complaint Center
identities.description: Mentioned as a channel through which the FBI typically releases IOCs associated with threats.

infrastructure.name: First VPN Service Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The network infrastructure of the "First VPN Service", used by threat actors to route malicious traffic, including C2 for botnets, and to obscure their true location.

indicators.name: Azure AD Sign-in from Known Anonymizing Service
indicators.pattern:
title: Azure AD Sign-in from Known Anonymizing Service
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e4f-4d2c-9a1b-7f6d5e4c3b2a
status: experimental
description: Detects Azure AD (Entra ID) sign-ins originating from IP addresses associated with known VPN, proxy, or other anonymizing services. This may indicate an attempt to bypass conditional access policies or hide the true origin of an attack.
references:
  - https://www.industrialcyber.co/news/fbi-links-first-vpn-service-to-ransomware-gangs-botnets-criminal-dark-web-activity-calls-for-layered-defensive-controls/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-27
modified: 2026-05-27
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.t1090
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.defense_evasion
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    # This rule requires a threat intelligence feed of known anonymizer IP ranges.
    # The IPAddress field should be checked against that list.
    # The following is a placeholder for the logic.
    IPAddress|cidr:
      - 198.51.100.0/24 # Example range for a known VPN provider
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate users using consumer VPNs for privacy, if not against corporate policy.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects authentication attempts from IP addresses associated with known anonymizing services like the 'First VPN Service', as recommended for monitoring by the FBI.

relationships.source_name: Azure AD Sign-in from Known Anonymizing Service
Azure AD Sign-in from Known Anonymizing Service
